title: Proxy Direct Messaging
description: Agent sends, polls, acknowledges, and lists direct messages via the DmHandler extension within the Proxy.

actors.name: Agent
actors.description: The consuming agent/client that calls proxy HTTP endpoints to manage direct messages

actors.name: DmHandler
actors.description: Extension class that wraps the store to provide DM-specific operations (send, poll, ack, list)

actors.name: Store
actors.description: Underlying message store; DmHandler delegates all persistence to store.send / store.poll / store.ack / store.list

use_cases.name: Send Direct Message
flow: Route validates recipient_node_id and content (400 if missing)
Route calls dmHandler.send({ recipientNodeId, content, metadata })
DmHandler calls store.send({ type:'dm', payload:{ recipient_node_id, content, metadata, sent_at }, priority:'normal' })
use_cases.fallback: If dmHandler is absent, route calls store.send() directly with the same payload

use_cases.name: Poll Direct Messages
flow: DmHandler calls store.poll({ type:'dm', limit: limit || 20 })
use_cases.fallback: If dmHandler is absent, route calls store.poll({ type:'dm', limit }) directly

use_cases.name: Acknowledge Messages
use_cases.trigger: DmHandler.ack(messageIds) — called internally (no dedicated /dm/ack route found; mailbox ack at POST /mailbox/ack uses store.ack directly)

use_cases.name: List Direct Messages
flow: DmHandler calls store.list({ type:'dm', limit: limit || 20, offset: offset || 0 })
use_cases.fallback: If dmHandler is absent, route calls store.list({ type:'dm', limit, offset }) directly

DmHandler.instantiation: new DmHandler({ store, logger }) in src/proxy/index.js (EvoMapProxy.start)

key_relationships: Agent uses DmHandler (via HTTP routes in routes.js)
DmHandler delegates_to Store (composition via injected store)
routes.js depends_on DmHandler (optional — falls back to store if absent)
http_endpoints: POST /dm/send   — send a DM to a recipient node
POST /mailbox/ack — acknowledge messages by ID (uses store directly)
important_notes: DmHandler is an optional extension; all four routes have fallback paths that call the store directly if dmHandler is not injected. The ack operation is exposed at /mailbox/ack (not /dm/ack) and bypasses DmHandler entirely, going straight to store.ack(). message type discriminator is always 'dm'.
